---
title: "Hylaea Miller"
image: images/image_park.JPEG
toc: false

about: 
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 26em
  links: 
    - text: LinkedIn
      icon: Linkedin
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/hylaea-miller/
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      href: https://github.com/hylaea-miller
    - text: Email
      href: mailto:hylaea@ucsb.edu
---

### Profile

Hi there, I'm Hylaea Miller! I’m a Master’s student in Environmental Data Science at the Bren School of Environmental Science & Management at UCSB. I have experience in GIS, Remote Sensing, Environmental Systems Modeling, technical documentation, data visualization, and programming in Python and R.

::: panel-tabset
### Education

**Master, Environmental Data Science** (June 2026) \| Bren School of Environmental Science & Management \| University of California, Santa Barbara (UCSB)

**Bachelor in Environmental Sciences** (October 2024) \| University of Brasília (UnB), Brazil

### Professional Experience

**Environmental Temporary Agent**\
Chico Mendes Institute for Biodiversity Conservation (ICMBio)\
*January 2024 - July 2025 \| Brasília, Brazil*

**GIS Intern**\
Chico Mendes Institute for Biodiversity Conservation (ICMBio)\
*June 2023 - December 2023 \| Brasília, Brazil*

**Environmental Research Intern**\
Institute of Research and Statistics of the Federal District (IPEDF)\
*March 2023 - April 2023 \| Brasília, Brazil*

### Project/Research

**Turning Data into Action. An R Package for the Wildfire Resilience Index** \| Master’s Capstone (Current) \| Collaborators: National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis (NCEAS) \| [Repository](https://github.com/ignitR-package)

**Detection of changes in land use in the AMACRO and MATOPIBA special territories** \| Undergraduate Thesis \| [Revista Brasileira de Geomática(RBGEO)](https://periodicos.utfpr.edu.br/rbgeo/article/view/19946) \| Advisor: Gustavo Macedo de Mello Baptista, PhD.

**Analysis of Satellite‑Derived Rainfall Data in Watersheds of the Cerrado Biome** \| Undergraduate Research Fellowship (2024) \| Advisor: Rejane Ennes Cicerelli, Ph.D.
:::
